title: Methods and systems for coating a steel substrate
abstract: The present disclosure provides systems and methods for depositing a metal layer adjacent to or on a substrate. Substrates may comprise, for example, one or more of iron, chromium, nickel, silicon, vanadium, titanium, boron, tungsten, aluminum, molybdenum, cobalt, manganese, zirconium, and niobium, oxides thereof, nitrides thereof, sulfides thereof, or any combination thereof. A substrate may be a steel substrate. A metal layer may be deposited via, for example, roll coating, vapor deposition, slurry deposition, or electrochemical deposition.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for forming at least one metal layer adjacent to a substrate, comprising: (a) providing said substrate having a grain size that is from about 1 International Association for Testing and Materials (ASTM) to 30 ASTM, wherein said substrate has an austenite to ferrite ratio of at least 1 as measured by x-ray diffraction spectroscopy, and wherein said substrate includes at least two of (i) carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt %, (ii) manganese from about 0.1 wt% to 3 wt %, (iii) silicon at less than or equal to about 1 wt %, (iv) vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%, and (v) titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%; (b) depositing a metal-containing layer adjacent to said substrate; and (c) annealing said substrate and said at least one metal-containing layer at an annealing temperature greater than 25 Â°C for an annealing time of at least about 1 second, thereby forming said at least one metal layer adjacent to said substrate, wherein said at least one metal layer has a grain size from about 1 ASTM to 30 ASTM. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes at least three of (i) carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt %, (ii) about 0.1 wt% to 3 wt % manganese, (iii) silicon at less than or equal to about 1 wt %, (iv) vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%, and (v) titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein said substrate includes at least four of (i) carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt %, (ii) about 0.1 wt% to 3 wt % manganese, (iii) silicon at less than or equal to about 1 wt %, (iv) vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%, and (v) titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes carbon at less than or equal to about 0.2 wt%. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein said substrate includes carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein said substrate includes carbon at less than or equal to about 0.05 wt%. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.1 wt% to about 2 wt% manganese. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes niobium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.001 wt% to about 0.01 wt% phosphorus. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.0001 wt% to about 0.01 wt% sulfur. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.001 wt% to about 0.1 wt% aluminum. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.001 wt% to about 0.2 wt% copper. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.001 wt% to about 0.1 wt% nickel. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.001 wt% to about 0.1 wt% chromium. 17. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.001 wt% to about 0.1 wt% molybdenum. 18. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.0001 wt% to about 0.01 wt% tin. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes about 0.0001 wt% to about 0.001 wt% colbalt. 20. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes nitrogen at less than or equal to about 0.01 wt%. 21. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes nitrogen at less than or equal to about 0.005 wt%. 22. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate includes titanium nitride. 23. The method of claim 1, wherein said depositing is by vapor deposition. 24. The method of claim 1, wherein said depositing is by electrochemical deposition. 25. The method of claim 1, wherein said depositing is by slurry deposition. 26. The method of claim 1, wherein said depositing is at a temperature from about 0 Â°C to 1000 Â°C. 27. The method of claim 1, wherein said depositing occurs in an atmosphere with levels of moisture below about 10 torr. 28. The method of claim 1, wherein the method further comprises heating at a rate of about 0.1 Â°C per second. 29. The method of claim 1, wherein said annealing is at a temperature above about 500 Â°C. 30. The method of claim 1, further comprising cooling of said substrate after said annealing. 31. The method of claim 1, wherein said substrate transitions from ferrite to austenite during said heating. 32. The method of claim 1, wherein said heating temperature is determined by a transition temperature at which ferrite transitions to austenite. 33. The method of claim 1, wherein addition of at least one austenite stabilizer lowers said transition temperature. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein said at least one austenite stabilizer is chosen from the group consisting of manganese, nitrogen, copper and gold. 35. The method of claim 1, wherein said at least one metal layer adjacent to said substrate has a ferrite grain size from about ASTM 1 to about ASTM 30. 36. The method of claim 1, comprising repeating step c. 37. The method of claim 1, further comprising drying said substrate. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein said drying occurs in an atmosphere of an inert gas. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein said inert gas is hydrogen, helium, argon, nitrogen, or any combination thereof. 40. The method of claim 1, wherein said at least one metal-containing layer comprises an alloying agent, a metal halide activator, and a solvent. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein said alloying agent is selected from ferrosilicon (FeSi), ferrochrome (FeCr), chromium, and combinations thereof. 42. The method of claim 40, wherein said metal halide activator includes a monovalent metal, a divalent metal or a trivalent metal. 43. The method of claim 42, wherein said metal halide activator is selected from the group consisting of magnesium chloride (MgCl 2 ), iron (II) chloride (FeCl 2 ), calcium chloride (CaCl 2 ), zirconium (IV) chloride (ZrCU), titanium (IV) chloride (TiCU), niobium (V) chloride (NbCU), titanium (III) chloride (TiCU), silicon tetrachloride (SiCU), vanadium (III) chloride (VCU), chromium (III) chloride (CrCU), trichlorosilance (SiHC13), manganese (II) chloride (MnCl 2 ), chromium (II) chloride (CrCl 2 ), cobalt (II) chloride (CoCl 2 ), copper (II) chloride (CuCl 2 ), nickel (II) chloride (NiCl 2 ), vanadium (II) chloride (VC1 2 ), ammonium chloride (NH 4 C1), sodium chloride (NaCl), potassium chloride (KC1), molybdenum sulfide (MoS), manganese sulfide 

 (MnS), iron disulfide (FeS 2 ), chromium sulfide (CrS), iron sulfide (FeS), copper sulfide (CuS), nickel sulfide (NiS) and combinations thereof. 44. The method of claim 40, wherein said solvent is an aqueous solvent. 45. The method of claim 40, wherein said solvent is an organic solvent. 46. The method of claim 1, wherein said at least one metal layer comprises an alloying agent and a metal halide activator. 47. The method of claim 40, wherein said alloying agent is selected from ferrosilicon (FeSi), ferrochrome (FeCr), chromium, and combinations thereof. 48. The method of claim 40, wherein said metal halide activator includes a monovalent metal, a divalent metal or a trivalent metal. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein said metal halide activator is selected from the group consisting of magnesium chloride (MgCl 2 ), iron (II) chloride (FeCl 2 ), calcium chloride (CaCl 2 ), zirconium (IV) chloride (ZrCU), titanium (IV) chloride (TiCU), niobium (V) chloride (NbCl 5 ), titanium (III) chloride (TiCl 3 ), silicon tetrachloride (S1CI 4 ), vanadium (III) chloride (VCI 3 ), chromium (III) chloride (CrCl 3 ), trichlorosilance (SiHC13), manganese (II) chloride (MnCl 2 ), chromium (II) chloride (CrCl 2 ), cobalt (II) chloride (CoCl 2 ), copper (II) chloride (CuCl 2 ), nickel (II) chloride (NiCl 2 ), vanadium (II) chloride (VC1 2 ), ammonium chloride (NH 4 CI), sodium chloride (NaCl), potassium chloride (KC1), molybdenum sulfide (MoS), manganese sulfide (MnS), iron disulfide (FeS 2 ), chromium sulfide (CrS), iron sulfide (FeS), copper sulfide (CuS), nickel sulfide (NiS) and combinations thereof. 50. A method for providing a steel substrate, comprising selecting said ferrite stabilizer such that a grain size of said steel substrate is about 7 per the International Association for Testing and Materials. 51. The method of claim 50, wherein said substrate includes carbon at less than or equal to about 0.2 wt%. 52. The method of claim 50, wherein said substrate includes about 0.1 wt% to about 2 wt% manganese. 53. The method of claim 50, wherein said substrate includes niobium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%. 54. The method of claim 50, wherein said substrate includes vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%. 55. The method of claim 50, wherein said substrate includes titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%. 56. The method of claim 50, wherein said substrate includes about 0.001 wt% to about 0.1 wt% chromium. 57. The method of claim 50, wherein said substrate includes titanium nitride. 58. The method of claim 50, wherein a ferrite-austenite transition temperature is less than about 1000 Â°C. 59. A part comprising an inner core and an external metallic layer adjacent to said inner core, wherein said inner core comprises a substrate containing ferrite grains and said external metallic layer comprises a metal alloy, wherein said substrate comprises carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt% as measured by X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS), and wherein said external metallic layer has a grain size that is from about 1 International Association for Testing and Materials (ASTM) to 30 ASTM. 60. The part of claim 59, wherein said metal alloy comprises an alloying agent and a metal halide activator. 61. The part of claim 59, wherein said alloying agent is selected from ferrosilicon (FeSi), ferrochrome (FeCr), chromium, and combinations thereof. 62. The part of claim 59, wherein said metal halide activator includes a monovalent metal, a divalent metal or a trivalent metal. 63. The part of claim 62, wherein said metal halide activator is selected from the group consisting of magnesium chloride (MgCl 2 ), iron (II) chloride (FeCl 2 ), calcium chloride (CaCl 2 ), zirconium (IV) chloride (ZrCU), titanium (IV) chloride (TiCU), niobium (V) chloride (NbCl 5 ), titanium (III) chloride (TiCl 3 ), silicon tetrachloride (S1CI 4 ), vanadium (III) chloride (VCI 3 ), chromium (III) chloride (CrCl 3 ), trichlorosilance (SiHC13), manganese (II) chloride (MnCl 2 ), chromium (II) chloride (CrCl 2 ), cobalt (II) chloride (CoCl 2 ), copper (II) chloride (CuCl 2 ), nickel (II) chloride (NiCl 2 ), vanadium (II) chloride (VC1 2 ), ammonium chloride (NH 4 CI), sodium chloride (NaCl), potassium chloride (KC1), molybdenum sulfide (MoS), manganese sulfide (MnS), iron disulfide (FeS 2 ), chromium sulfide (CrS), iron sulfide (FeS), copper sulfide (CuS), nickel sulfide (NiS) and combinations thereof. 64. The part of claim 59, wherein said substrate includes at least two of (i) carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt %, (ii) manganese from about 0.1 wt% to 3 wt %, (iii) silicon at less than or equal to about 1 wt %, (iv) vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%, and (v) titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%. 65. The part of claim 59, wherein said substrate includes at least three of (i) carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt %, (ii) manganese from about 0.1 wt% to 3 wt %, (iii) silicon at less 

 than or equal to about 1 wt %, (iv) vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%, and (v) titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%. 66. The part of claim 65, wherein said substrate includes at least four of (i) carbon at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt %, (ii) manganese from about 0.1 wt% to 3 wt %, (iii) silicon at less than or equal to about 1 wt %, (iv) vanadium at less than or equal to about 0.1 wt%, and (v) titanium at less than or equal to about 0.5 wt%. 67. The part of claim 59, wherein said substrate has carbon at less than or equal to about 0.01% as measured by XPS.
